column1: # mapping_set_title: 'OCSF to CVE Record Format class mappings'
#   Class-level mappings between OCSF (Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework)
#   classes and the CVE Record Format v5 schema. OCSF has first-class CVE
#   support via the Cve object, and vulnerability-oriented event classes
#   (VulnerabilityFinding) that carry CVE identifiers and CVSS scores. The
#   AffectedPackage object corresponds directly to CVE AffectedProduct.

column8: OCSF Cve object is a direct representation of a CVE Program record; both carry CVE-ID, description, references, and CVSS scores.
OCSF Vulnerability wraps one or more CVE references; CVERecord is the authoritative CVE Program container — close but not exact because Vulnerability can reference non-CVE advisories.
Direct 1:1 — OCSF AffectedPackage and CVE AffectedProduct both record vendor/product/version triples for software impacted by a vulnerability.
A VulnerabilityFinding event reports on a detected CVE; the underlying data model aligns closely with CVERecord fields.
An OCSF Advisory (e.g. Microsoft KB, Apple Security Advisory) parallels a CVE CNA Published Container which holds the vendor-provided vulnerability details.
OCSF Cvss captures CVSS vector/score metadata; CVE CnaContainer aggregates CVSS metrics among other fields — related rather than equivalent.
